label: Reverse Mountain and Twin Cape voyage system

plan_scope.deliverable_boundary: Contract and story ledger only. This file does not promote a runtime asset or authorize app integration.
plan_scope.canon_policy: Local chapter summaries and canon records establish gates. Panel-level geometry that is not in those sources remains unresolved or default-hidden.

anchor.name: Reverse Mountain
anchor.japanese: リヴァース・マウンテン

atlas_anchor.warning: Never infer local canals, bearings, distances, or Twin Cape placement from this atlas point.

components.name: East Blue ascent current
components.geometry_warning: The source verifies travel up Reverse Mountain, not exact channel width, bends, banks, or bearings.

components.name: Grand Line descent current
components.geometry_warning: Exact descent shape and local bearing require panel verification.

atlas_anchor.warning: The deterministic atlas coordinate must not set its local position relative to Reverse Mountain.

components.name: Twin Cape lighthouse
components.geometry_warning: Dimensions, facade, and exact cape placement require visual reference review.

components.name: Going Merry
components.gate_warning: Do not reuse the approximate acquisition chapter from vessels.json as a verified canon gate. This contract only requires the ship to be present in chapters 100-105.

components.name: Unidentified giant whale
components.label_policy: Do not display the name Laboon at chapter 102.

components.name: Laboon interior theatre
components.geometry_warning: Use an authored inset; do not claim anatomical scale or internal topology without reference verification.

components.name: Local whirlpool emitter
components.gate_warning: The local chapter summaries do not establish a whirlpool in chapters 100-105. Build the reusable control, but never enable it in this voyage until panel-level evidence establishes a gate.

topology.summary: A chapter-gated passage from the East Blue approach, up Reverse Mountain, across a crest transition, down into the Grand Line, and into the Twin Cape/Laboon theatre. Only the sequence is established here; local bearings and distances remain schematic.
topology.scale_policy: Use experiential readability and relative staging until panel-level proportions are approved; do not present authored meters as canon.

pre_reveal_guard.rule: The Reverse Mountain environment, Twin Cape, Laboon, and Crocus remain hidden. Chapter 100 may show only the Going Merry on open sea heading toward the Grand Line.

temporal_variants.state: Reverse Mountain and its uphill voyage route reveal while the Going Merry ascends toward the Grand Line.
Twin Cape becomes available and an unidentified giant whale swallows the Going Merry. Laboon's name and Crocus remain hidden.
The whale is identified as Laboon, Crocus appears, and the interior theatre can be entered.
The exterior Twin Cape theatre returns and Luffy's promise to Laboon becomes the active story beat.
Crocus gives the crew a Log Pose and the Going Merry departs with Mr. 9 and Miss Wednesday toward Whisky Peak.

event_scenes.state_delta: The world view hands off to a moving voyage inset as the Going Merry climbs the current and crests the Red Line.
event_scenes.camera_cue: Begin just above wave height behind the Merry, bank with the route, widen at the summit, and avoid revealing Twin Cape before the chapter-102 cut.

event_scenes.state_delta: The Merry reaches the Grand Line, encounters an unidentified giant whale, and transitions into the whale interior.
event_scenes.camera_cue: Level from the descent route, let the whale silhouette overwhelm the frame, then use mouth occlusion for a clean interior transition.

event_scenes.state_delta: Crocus and Laboon's identity become readable without changing the world-scale creature into a permanent open interior model.
event_scenes.camera_cue: Cut to a contained interior inset for the explanation, then return to the exterior Twin Cape scale shot.

event_scenes.state_delta: The promise becomes a persistent chapter-safe relationship state between Luffy and Laboon.
event_scenes.camera_cue: Hold Luffy and the Merry as readable foreground scale against Laboon, then end on Laboon's response rather than inventing extra combat.

event_scenes.state_delta: The Log Pose and two passengers establish the next voyage, then the Merry exits the local inset toward Whisky Peak.
event_scenes.camera_cue: Use Crocus and the Log Pose as the close insert, then pull behind the departing Merry and hand back to the globe route.

chapter_logic.default_rule: Render the latest verified state whose reveal chapter is less than or equal to the reader chapter.
chapter_logic.unknown_gate_rule: A null reveal_chapter or chapter_to_verify element stays default-hidden and cannot enter runtime promotion.

route_network.route_ownership: MapLibre owns voyage state and chapter gates. The local renderer samples normalized route progress against an authored inset spline.
route_network.spline_policy: Spline shape is cinematic scaffolding and must never be presented as panel-accurate cartography.
route_network.travel_interaction: Scrubbing chapters 101-105 selects the event state; replay animates the Merry but never advances canon state by itself.

creature.scale_strategy: full close rig plus reduced mid mesh plus globe silhouette impostor

actors.reason: A small readable performance does not justify a hero 3D character rig in W1.
Keep character likeness in the signed atlas lane while the ship, whale, and world remain 3D.

verified_fields.behavior: drives uphill surface flow, foam advection, spray, and Merry wake alignment
drives downhill route motion and exit wake; exact vector layout remains schematic
supports Laboon displacement, breath mist contact, and Merry idle/departure wakes

unverified_fields.behavior: reusable vortex vector field, foam ring, depression normal, spray orbit, and buoyancy perturbation

current_and_whirlpool_system.physics_policy: Art-directed deterministic vector fields drive visible motion. Full fluid simulation may produce offline reference but is not required at runtime.
current_and_whirlpool_system.buoyancy_policy: Vehicle and creature roots receive low-frequency authored heave/roll plus sampled wave height; chapter playback remains deterministic.

lod_program.principle: Environment, vehicle, creature, and effects degrade independently so Laboon's scale remains readable without loading the close inset.

tiers.use: event replay and Unreal close exploration
tiers.effects: full masks, wake, spray, mist, and interior inset
reduced particles, full silhouette, no interior loaded unless chapter 103 scene opens
single wake ribbon, foam hint, breath billboard

tiers.use: unsupported WebGL, distant globe, or reduced motion
tiers.effects: chapter-safe route line and signed transparent silhouettes

texture_budget.unreal_source_textures: retain higher-resolution masters outside web export
texture_budget.compression: KTX2/Basis for web; Unreal platform compression after import

fallbacks.reduced_motion: Freeze vehicle and creature on a verified chapter tableau; keep current flow to a low-amplitude shader or disable it.
fallbacks.webgl_unavailable: Use a transparent signed environment plate, Merry silhouette, whale silhouette, and MapLibre route animation.
fallbacks.asset_load_failure: Keep the chapter marker and route available; never substitute a later ship, named Laboon label at chapter 102, or unrelated sea creature.
fallbacks.audio_unavailable: Run the deterministic visual timeline silently. Audio cannot own event timing.
fallbacks.unverified_geometry: Use broad silhouette and mist occlusion rather than inventing detailed canal, lighthouse, or whale-interior topology.

runtime_ownership.procedural_ocean_fx: shared effect definitions interpreted by web shader/particles or Unreal materials/Niagara
runtime_ownership.audio: audio cue registry; licensed or original effects only
runtime_ownership.lifetime_rule: MapLibre/app state creates, gates, pauses, and disposes local scene objects; the Three.js layer cannot advance or infer reader chapter.
runtime_ownership.determinism_rule: Given chapter, scene id, route_t, and playback time, vehicle, creature, camera, and FX state must be reproducible.

unreal_reuse.goal: The web proof remains the source of narrative behavior while neutral assets and masks import into an Unreal exploration prototype without remodelling.
source_rules: Use one-meter source scale, named roots, applied transforms, stable material slots, and semantic node names.
Keep environment, Going Merry, Laboon, interior inset, route splines, and effects as separate deliverables.
Export skeletal meshes and named clips independently from environment geometry.
Keep high-resolution textures and mask masters outside the compressed web package.
Do not bake chapter logic, map coordinates, or audio ownership into Blender drivers.
unreal_reuse.web_export: glTF/GLB plus route metadata, compressed textures, and signed fallbacks
unreal_reuse.unreal_export: Use Unreal's supported interchange path for the neutral mesh/rig source; an import adapter handles axis conversion and maps semantic IDs into Data Assets.

unreal_mapping.spray_wake_mist: Niagara systems driven by shared masks and sockets
unreal_mapping.chapter_state: Data Asset keyed by the same event and component IDs
unreal_mapping.lods: Static Mesh and Skeletal Mesh LOD chains

unreal_reuse.acceptance: A clean import recognizes semantic roots, LODs, material slots, Laboon skeleton, Going Merry root animation, and every required named clip without hand-renaming.

coordinate_policy.atlas_anchor: Use Reverse Mountain's app coordinate only to place or open the local voyage scene on the globe.
coordinate_policy.local_topology: Build only from cited order and containment evidence. Never infer local bearings, distances, canal shape, or Twin Cape placement from deterministic atlas jitter.
coordinate_policy.route_coordinates: Store local normalized spline points in asset space; MapLibre owns the world route and provides route progress.
coordinate_policy.runtime: Use an expanded local inset for the ascent, Laboon first contact, and Twin Cape; return to the globe for the chapter-105 departure.

visual_program.master_scene: One Blender source package with linked, separately exportable environment, route, vehicle, creature, event-state, FX-control, and LOD collections.
must_show: Going Merry as a moving vehicle rather than a baked part of the mountain
a readable uphill current and crest transition at chapter 101
Laboon's overwhelming scale at chapter 102 without displaying the name early
a separate interior inset and Crocus performance at chapter 103
the Luffy/Laboon promise as the chapter-104 emotional endpoint
the Log Pose and departure handoff at chapter 105
independent current, wake, spray, mist, creature, and vehicle controls
separate LODs and signed fallbacks for web and Unreal reuse
must_not_show: Reverse Mountain, Twin Cape, Laboon, or Crocus before their chapter gates
the name Laboon during the chapter-102 unidentified-whale state
a permanently active whirlpool unsupported by local chapter evidence
local bearings or distances inferred from atlas coordinates
a full detailed whale interior attached to every exterior Laboon LOD
Crocus or Straw Hat likenesses replaced with generic low-quality polygon people
later Straw Hat vessels or post-timeskip costumes
copyrighted anime music or dialogue bundled into the asset
visual_program.runtime_handoff: Export each asset family separately. App state controls anchor, chapter gate, route progress, active event, camera cue, and disposal.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: The story ledger contains exactly one planned row for every chapter from 100 through 105, and every row pins the current local chapter description hash.
At chapter 100, all Reverse Mountain, Twin Cape, Laboon, and Crocus geometry and labels are absent.
At chapter 101, the ascent can run but Twin Cape, Laboon, and Crocus remain hidden.
At chapter 102, the giant whale can appear and swallow the Merry, but its UI label is Unidentified giant whale and Crocus remains hidden.
At chapters 103, 104, and 105, the Laboon/Crocus reveal, promise, and Log Pose departure activate in order without deleting earlier persistent geography.
Environment, landmark, route, Going Merry, Laboon, event-state, and FX assets are independently addressable and independently unloadable.
All required Going Merry and Laboon clips exist by exact name, have deterministic duration, and do not contain the world-route transform baked into the rig.
Verified current fields run at their gates; the whirlpool field ships disabled and cannot be enabled without a resolved chapter gate.
All three 3D tiers respect their per-asset triangle budgets and LOD3 renders a chapter-safe fallback with no later-state label leakage.
Two identical inputs of chapter, scene id, route_t, and playback time produce the same camera, transforms, FX state, and active labels.
The neutral package imports with stable scale, roots, material slots, LODs, masks, and named clips; chapter logic remains in an Unreal Data Asset/controller rather than in the mesh.
Reduced-motion, asset-failure, WebGL-unavailable, and audio-unavailable paths preserve chapter order and remain usable.

unresolved: Panel-verified shape, count, width, and bearings of Reverse Mountain's visible waterways.
Panel-verified dimensions and placement of the crest, Twin Cape shoreline, and lighthouse.
Panel-verified Laboon proportions, color blocking, eye/mouth rig range, and exterior marks for the chapter-102 through chapter-104 window.
Panel-verified interior Laboon layout and transition framing.
Whether any whirlpool is visible in chapters 100-105; keep the emitter disabled until verified.
Exact panel timing and choreography within each chapter; chapter summaries establish beats but not shot order.
Going Merry acquisition gate in vessels.json remains marked verified:false and is outside this W1 chapter-window contract.
Licensed/original audio cue selection for current roar, hull strain, Laboon breath, swallow transition, and lighthouse ambience.

used_for: Reverse Mountain to Whisky Peak journey ordering
